transcript.text: I just got into a big fight with my parents about my grades and what I want to do after high school. Someone on Instagram said something about my weight and it's making me feel like I don't fit in. Now I have a graphic novel due in a few days and I'm just stuck, like I can't even start.

You're facing a really tight deadline for your graphic novel, and on top of that, you've just had a big fight with your parents and are dealing with a hurtful comment about your weight. It's no wonder you're feeling stuck and overwhelmed. Can you tell me more about what's been the hardest part of starting this project, is it the pressure from your parents, the self-doubt, or something else entirely?
Honestly, it's all of it. The parents are yelling, the comment makes me feel awful, and I just can't even pick up a pen because I don't even trust my own drawing anymore.

The deadline for your graphic novel is just a few days away, and on top of that, you're dealing with the weight of your parents' expectations and the sting of that hurtful comment. It's like the pressure is suffocating your creativity, and you can't even pick up a pen because you're doubting your own abilities. Yeah, it's a lot to handle. Can you think of a small step you could take to just get started, even if it's just a tiny sketch or a few lines of dialogue?

I could try a quick sketch, but my brain feels like it's stuck on mute while everyone else is shouting. The last thing I want is another reminder that my art is judged just like my body. Maybe a five-minute doodle will do, or maybe it'll just push me deeper into procrastination.

You're feeling like you're stuck and can't even start working on your graphic novel because the pressure from the deadline and the fear of being judged are overwhelming you. It's like your brain is frozen, and the thought of taking that first step feels daunting. Yeah, it's a lot to handle. Maybe taking a tiny step, like that five-minute doodle, could help you get unstuck without feeling like you're committing to something that might be judged. What do you think might happen if you just let yourself doodle for a few minutes without any expectations?
I don't know if I even want to try. Maybe I'll just stare at my phone instead of drawing.

You're feeling really stuck right now, and the thought of starting your graphic novel is overwhelming. It's like the pressure from the deadline and the fear of being judged are suffocating your creativity. Yeah, it's a lot to handle. If you're willing, maybe taking a tiny step, like that five-minute doodle, could help you get unstuck without feeling like you're committing to something that might be judged. What do you think might happen if you just let yourself doodle for a few minutes without any expectations?
I don't think doodling will help; I'd probably just end up looking like a mess and my parents will still judge me. Maybe I'll just stare at my phone instead.

You're feeling really overwhelmed by the deadline and the fear of being judged, and it's like that's stopping you from even picking up a pen. It makes sense that you're worried your doodling will be a mess and that your parents will judge you - it's a lot of pressure to deal with. Yeah, it can be really tough to start something new when you're feeling that way. Maybe we can just sit with that feeling for a moment, and see if there's a small way to take the pressure off, even if it's just for a few minutes.

I don't know if I want to try that at all; maybe just scrolling through Insta will distract me from the whole mess.  I keep feeling like everyone is judging me, even my art, and that makes me want to put it all down.  Honestly, I don't even trust myself to finish this thing before the deadline.

You're feeling like you're under a microscope, and everyone's judgment is just too much to handle right now. It's like the fear of not being good enough is suffocating your creativity, and even the thought of picking up a pen feels daunting. I can see why scrolling through Instagram might seem like a tempting distraction, but maybe we can find a way to make that first step a little less scary. What's the smallest thing you could do to start working on your graphic novel, even if it's just for a minute?

I don't even want to try that. I'll just keep scrolling and maybe find a meme about how everyone judges me after that fight with my parents. I just feel like there's no point, everyone is looking at me.

You're feeling like you're at a dead end, and the thought of working on your graphic novel just feels like too much right now. It's like the weight of everyone's expectations, including your parents', is crushing your creativity. I can see why you'd think that scrolling through Instagram might be a way to escape, but it's also possible that it's just a temporary distraction from the feelings that are really bothering you. What's really going on is that you're scared of not being good enough, and that fear is paralyzing you. Yeah, it's a really tough spot to be in.
I just want to lie in bed and not think about it. Everyone's staring at me, and I hate how my body feels.

You're feeling like you're under so much scrutiny, and it's like everyone's eyes are on you, making you hate how your body feels. Your anxiety about your body image is really overwhelming you right now, and it's understandable that you'd want to escape it by lying in bed. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge these feelings, and I'm here to support you. Can you tell me more about what's been going on that's making you feel like everyone's staring at you?

I keep thinking everyone's watching me, especially after the fight with my parents. I can't even look in the mirror without feeling judged, so I just scroll through Insta to avoid it. It feels like I'm stuck and everyone is staring at me.

You're feeling like you're under a microscope, and it's overwhelming to deal with the constant feeling of being watched, especially after that fight with your parents. It's like every glance in the mirror becomes a judgment, and even Instagram, which might seem like a distraction, is actually a reminder of the body image concerns that are bothering you. It takes a lot of courage to admit how stuck you're feeling right now. Yeah, it's really tough to face these feelings, but maybe we can explore what's driving this need to escape and find a way to make it feel safer for you to confront your body image concerns.
I heard my parents yelling again, so I just keep scrolling instead of looking at myself. It feels like nobody cares and I'm stuck.

The sound of your parents yelling again must have been really tough to hear, and it's no wonder you felt like scrolling through Instagram was a way to escape. It's like that noise just confirmed your worst fears - that nobody cares and you're stuck. But in this moment, I want you to know that I'm here, and I care. Can you tell me more about what happens when you hear your parents yelling, and how that makes you feel about yourself?

response.final_response: The sound of your parents yelling again must have been really tough to hear, and it's no wonder you felt like scrolling through Instagram was a way to escape. It's like that noise just confirmed your worst fears - that nobody cares and you're stuck. But in this moment, I want you to know that I'm here, and I care. Can you tell me more about what happens when you hear your parents yelling, and how that makes you feel about yourself?
